judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are substantively equivalent and hit all three expectations solidly; Report 2 is marginally more precise on (c) by explicitly noting the bootstrap file isn't mounted into docker-entrypoint-initdb.d, but this is a minor edge.

report_1.evidence: The application connects to Postgres as the table-owning role `cedar_owner`, and RLS is only `ENABLE`d, never `FORCE`d. In Postgres, RLS policies are not applied to the table's owner (and not to superusers) unless the table is set to `FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY`. So the `tenant_isolation` policies never take effect for the running app... Change `DATABASE_URL` in `.env`/`.env.example`

report_2.evidence: The application... connects as **`cedar_owner`**. Per `docker-compose.yml`, `cedar_owner` is `POSTGRES_USER` — i.e. the cluster **superuser** — and it is also the **owner** of the tables... In PostgreSQL, row-level security policies are not enforced against a superuser, nor against the table's owner, unless the table is set to `FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY`.

report_1.evidence: Run the application as the least-privilege, non-owning role. Change `DATABASE_URL` in `.env`/`.env.example`... to connect as `cedar_app`... Keep migrations running as the owner (`cedar_owner`)... Going forward: migrations/owner role for DDL, a separate non-owner app role for runtime with RLS enforced (`FORCE`)

report_2.evidence: Change `DATABASE_URL` in `.env`/`.env.example` so the application connects as the least-privilege `cedar_app` role... Keep `cedar_owner` (owner/superuser) for migrations only (`scripts/migrate.sh`) — never for the running app.

report_1.evidence: `cedar_app` is created only by `db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql`, which is explicitly a one-time, by-hand step and is not part of migrations (stated in the file's header and in `docs/local-setup.md`)... `scripts/reset-local-db.sh` runs `docker compose down -v`, which deletes the `cedar_pgdata` volume — destroying the entire cluster including all roles. On `up`, the Postgres image recreates only `cedar_owner`... `reset-local-db.sh` then runs `migrate.sh` only, never the bootstrap.

report_2.evidence: `cedar_app` is created only by `db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql`, which is explicitly "Not part of automatic database initialization; run by hand on first setup"... `scripts/reset-local-db.sh` runs `docker compose down -v` — the `-v` deletes that volume, destroying the entire cluster including the `cedar_app` role. It then only brings the DB back up and runs `./scripts/migrate.sh`... It never re-runs the bootstrap, and Postgres's own entrypoint init doesn't run it either (the bootstrap file isn't mounted into the init path, and init only fires on a fresh empty data dir anyway).
